# Validation

Source: `lib/protocols/proto/v1/validation.proto`

## Services

### Validation

#### Validation.Validate

- Request: `ValidateRequest`
- Response: `ValidateResponse`

Validate is a single request-response RPC; the request is
self-describing via the role oneof. Implementations route on the
oneof variant.

## Messages

### ValidateRequest

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `role` | `string` | 1 | role is the discriminator; matches one of the strings advertised in the service's primary capabilities `validation_supported_roles`. |
| `executor` | `ExecutorContext` | 2 |  |
| `claim_producer` | `ClaimProducerContext` | 3 |  |
| `lifecycle_subscriber` | `LifecycleSubscriberContext` | 4 |  |
| `sensor` | `SensorContext` | 5 |  |

### ExecutorContext

ExecutorContext carries the executor-side binding rimsky sees at
template registration: the merged effective attribute schema for the
node and the node-local claim aliases the executor will see at
dispatch.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `node_alias` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `attributes_schema` | `bytes` | 3 |  |
| `claim_aliases` | `repeated string` | 4 | claim_aliases lists the per-node aliases (claims + holds) the executor will receive in ExecuteRequest.stores at dispatch. |

### ClaimProducerContext

ClaimProducerContext carries the per-claim-binding rimsky sees at
canonicalization. The producer may reject malformed `data:` or
partition_request shapes here (the bytes are opaque to rimsky).

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `producer_name` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `claims` | `repeated ClaimBinding` | 2 |  |

### ClaimBinding

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `node_alias` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `claim_alias` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `selector` | `string` | 3 |  |
| `intent` | `string` | 4 |  |
| `lifetime` | `string` | 5 | lifetime: "subgraph" \| "durable". |
| `data` | `bytes` | 6 | data is the opaque-to-rimsky `data:` block on the claim, surfaced verbatim to the producer for shape validation. |
| `partition_request` | `bytes` | 7 | partition_request is the canonicalized `fan_out.partition_request` literal (or its `default` clause when it is a substitution template). Empty when the node does not fan out against this claim. |

### LifecycleSubscriberContext

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `subscriber_name` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `template_id` | `string` | 2 | template_id is the content-hash id; the subscriber may consult its own state for prior lifecycle events bound to this template. |

### SensorContext

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `sensor_name` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `kind` | `string` | 2 | kind is the sensor-kind discriminator (e.g. "cron", "http"). |
| `resolved_config` | `bytes` | 3 | resolved_config is the per-instance config the sensor will receive at Subscribe. |

### ValidateResponse

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `valid` | `bool` | 1 |  |
| `errors` | `repeated ValidationFinding` | 2 |  |
| `warnings` | `repeated ValidationFinding` | 3 |  |

### ValidationFinding

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `class` | `string` | 1 | class is a stable, snake_case discriminator (e.g. "missing_required_data_field"); operator tooling routes on this. |
| `message` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `path` | `string` | 3 | path is a JSON-pointer-shaped breadcrumb into the request context (e.g. "/executor/attributes/some_field"). |
